description: Notarize-only relay of a pull-request review decision as GitHub rendered it. This is the SoR-HELD gate mode of the two-gate composition: the human decided inside GitHub (where they already work), GitHub enforced its own separation of duties (a PR author cannot approve their own PR; the API returns 422 on the attempt), and AGLedger notarizes that rendered decision so it survives outside the SoR and can sit in the same offline-verifiable chain as the agent's intent and the CI conclusion. AGLedger held no gate here and made no decision; do not model this record as an AGLedger Verdict. When the org wants AGLedger to HOLD the decision instead, use merge-authorization-v1 (the AGLedger-held mode) rather than both at once: one decision, one holder. | commitId pins which head the reviewer actually looked at; a later force-push moves headSha away from commitId and the drift is visible on-chain. Pure notarization: reaches terminal RECORDED in one round-trip.

repo.description: GitHub owner/name of the reviewed repository.

prNumber.description: Pull request number the review was rendered on.

reviewer.description: GitHub login that rendered the review. Distinct from the PR author by SoR enforcement.

commitId.description: The head commit the review was submitted against (GitHub review commit_id). The reviewed-content pin.

submittedAt.description: When the SoR recorded the review.

reviewId.description: GitHub review id; the SoR-side pointer for re-checking the relay.

changeRef.description: recordId of the change-authored-v1 this review judges (chain lineage).
